commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` for listing configured hosted marketplace feed entries, including `--feed-profile`, `--feed-url`, `--offline`, and `--json` modes.

PR surface: Source +115, Tests +217, Docs +14. Total +346 across 7 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR finding: source inspection follows `--feed-url` into metadata/error output, and a Node fetch probe confirms credential-bearing URL errors include the full URL. This is not a current-main bug because the `entries` command does not exist on current main.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR is draft, maintainer-labeled, explicitly paused for stack shaping, has missing contributor real-behavior proof, and has a security-sensitive output finding that should be handled in normal maintainer review.

target.title: Add marketplace feed entries command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Full hosted marketplace feed stack

We are pausing this stack at PR8 for Vincent's review and takeover/shaping before wiring hosted marketplace data into startup, install, or automatic refresh behavior.

- PR1 [#95846](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95846): refactors the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
- PR2 [#95868](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95868): adds the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
- PR3 [#95877](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95877): adds accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
- PR4 [#95964](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95964): persists hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
- PR5 [#95969](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95969): adds marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
- PR6 [#95981](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/95981): adds configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
- PR7 [#96155](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96155): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
- PR8 [#96158](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/96158): adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.

Next after review, not in this stack yet: decide whether PR9 should wire marketplace entries into install/search/startup, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Summary

This is PR8 in the hosted OpenClaw marketplace feed stack. It adds the first explicit read path over the configured hosted marketplace feed: `openclaw plugins marketplace entries`.

The command lists normalized entries from the configured/default OpenClaw marketplace feed and reports whether the data came from a fresh hosted payload, an accepted hosted snapshot, or bundled fallback data. It also supports `--offline` so maintainers can prove the refreshed snapshot path without fetching the live feed.

## Stack context

- RFC: openclaw/rfcs#19 defines the hosted marketplace feed direction.
- PR1: shape the bundled external plugin catalog as feed-like data.
- PR2: lazy hosted feed fetch from the configured public marketplace feed.
- PR3: hosted snapshot fallback.
- PR4: persistent snapshot storage in OpenClaw state.
- PR5: source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
- PR6: configurable marketplace feed/source profiles.
- PR7: `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` manual snapshot refresh.
- PR8: this PR, `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` manual feed/snapshot listing.

## What changed

- Adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` with `--feed-profile`, `--feed-url`, `--offline`, and `--json`.
- Adds an explicit `offline` mode to the hosted catalog loader so callers can read the latest accepted snapshot without doing a network request.
- Normalizes listed entries into id, label, kind, package/version, and install metadata.
- Updates the plugin CLI docs to include `marketplace entries` and `marketplace refresh`.
- Adds focused CLI and hosted catalog tests for parser dispatch, JSON output, bundled fallback output, and offline snapshot reuse.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/cli/plugins.md src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-marketplace-refresh`

## Review note

The final Codex review reported no discrete correctness issues. Its internal sandbox could not run tests because the read-only review sandbox could not create the heavy-check lock directory, but the local targeted tests above passed.
